title: quercus virginiana f. virescens
intro: Quercus virginiana f. virescens, also known as the Live Oak, is a variety of the Live Oak tree (Quercus virginiana) that is native to the southeastern United States. The medicinal properties of Quercus virginiana f. virescens include its high tannin content, which has been used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and inflammation. The health benefits of Quercus virginiana f. virescens are thought to include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. Herbal preparations of Quercus virginiana f. virescens, such as teas and tinctures, are often used to promote digestive health and reduce inflammation.
